questions.question: Define compactness in a metric space and provide an example to illustrate your answer.
questions.hint: Compactness is a property of a metric space where every open cover has a finite subcover.

questions.question: Prove that a metric space is compact if and only if it is complete and totally bounded.
questions.hint: Use the definitions of compactness, completeness, and total boundedness to prove the statement.

questions.question: Show that the closed unit ball in a complete metric space is compact by using the Heine-Borel theorem.
questions.hint: Apply the Heine-Borel theorem to the closed unit ball in the complete metric space.
